serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Belgian waffle served with maple syrup or chocolate sauce, approximately 150 g, representing a typical single-serve dessert or snack portion as sold at food service outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Waffles, plain, frozen, ready-to-heat (SR Legacy ID 18369) and Waffles, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18368)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Belgian waffle as sold in Singapore food service outlets is not individually listed in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy waffle entries (plain, prepared from recipe) and cross-referenced with the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The input values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for plain waffles with added sugar toppings. Sodium is elevated relative to plain waffle (233 mg/100g) consistent with commercial preparation. Values retained from input and documented as adapted estimates.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Belgian waffle with chocolate sauce or maple syrup exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is taken from the closest analogous entry: waffles (plain, made from mix), GI=76 (glucose reference), as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI Tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (76 × 38.67 g/100g × 150 g serving / 100) / 100 = (76 × 58.0 g) / 100 ≈ 44.1 g GL. Toppings (maple syrup, chocolate sauce) would further elevate GL in practice.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (20.0 g/100g) with low fibre (1.33 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; saturated fat (8.0 g/100g) exceeds AHA threshold of 5 g/100g, contributing to arterial stiffness; sodium at 233 mg/100g adds direct pressor load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Belgian waffle consumption due to the combination of high sugar, high saturated fat, and moderate sodium, all of which independently elevate blood pressure; if consumed, omit high-sugar toppings and limit portion to no more than half a waffle.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Total carbohydrate 38.67 g/100g with sugar 20.0 g/100g and dietary fibre only 1.33 g/100g produces a high glycaemic index (GI 76) and very high glycaemic load per serving (~44 g GL), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Belgian waffle as a routine food; the high GI (76), very high GL per serving (~44), and low fibre content will cause significant postprandial hyperglycaemia, and maple syrup or chocolate sauce toppings further compound glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 8.0 g/100g (exceeds AHA limit of 5–6 g/100g for CVD patients) and trans fat at 0.33 g/100g both raise LDL cholesterol; cholesterol content is 66.67 mg/100g; high sugar load promotes triglyceride synthesis.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Belgian waffle due to its high saturated fat (8.0 g/100g), presence of trans fat (0.33 g/100g), and high sugar content (20.0 g/100g), all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and increase atherosclerotic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (8.0 g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6); sugar (20.0 g/100g) exceeds RA refined sugar threshold of 15 g/100g, driving systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Belgian waffle as its high saturated fat and refined sugar content promote inflammatory mediators; substituting butter with olive oil and omitting high-sugar toppings would reduce but not eliminate inflammatory risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Belgian Waffle is a thick, grid-patterned batter cake cooked in a specialized waffle iron that creates deep square pockets with a crispy golden exterior and a soft, fluffy interior. Originating in Belgium and widely popularized globally, it is served as a dessert or snack in Singapore food service outlets, typically accompanied by maple syrup or chocolate sauce. The batter is made from wheat flour, eggs, butter, milk, sugar, and leavening agents. Per 100 g, it provides 300 kcal, 5.33 g protein, 13.33 g fat (of which 8.0 g is saturated fat and 0.33 g is trans fat), 38.67 g carbohydrate (of which 20.0 g is sugar), 1.33 g dietary fibre, and 233 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is high (GI 76), and a standard 150 g serving carries a glycaemic load of approximately 44, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. The high saturated fat content raises LDL cholesterol risk. The food is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant modification. Common modifications include wholegrain flour substitution, reduced butter, and fresh fruit toppings in place of syrup or chocolate sauce.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Belgian waffle?
Is Belgian waffle high in sugar?
Is Belgian waffle suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Belgian waffle as sold in Singapore food service is not individually listed in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a named entry. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy waffle (plain, prepared from recipe) and are consistent with the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The adaptation is defensible and documented. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entry (waffles, plain, GI 76, Atkinson et al. 2008). Medium confidence is appropriate given the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for Belgian waffle as prepared in Singapore food service are not individually reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy waffle entries (SR Legacy ID 18368/18369) or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024; the SR Legacy entries do not disaggregate monounsaturated fatty acids for this product category. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: same basis as monounsaturated_fat_g; polyunsaturated fatty acid breakdown not reported in USDA SR Legacy waffle entries or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium is not reported in the input record and is not available in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle entries used as the nutrient source for this record; HPB Singapore Foodo does not list Belgian waffle as of 2024. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: vitamin A data not reported in USDA SR Legacy waffle entries (SR Legacy ID 18368/18369) for this product category as of 2024. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg set to -1: thiamine data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg set to -1: riboflavin data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg set to -1: niacin data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_b6_mg set to -1: vitamin B6 data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_b12_mcg set to -1: vitamin B12 data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: folate data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: vitamin D data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: vitamin E data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. phosphorus_mg set to -1: phosphorus data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. magnesium_mg set to -1: magnesium data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. zinc_mg set to -1: zinc data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium data not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy waffle source entries as of 2024. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy waffle data; waffles contain negligible vitamin C. Input cultural metadata (primary_cuisine: Chinese, ethnic_tags: Chinese/Milk Tea/Tea, country_of_origin: Singapore) appears to be erroneous metadata from the source system and has been corrected: Belgian Waffle is a Western dessert of Belgian origin, widely sold in Singapore. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean food service context (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 rules. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (waffles, plain, made from mix); toppings would further elevate GL in practice. Trans fat 0.33 g/100g is retained from input; source is commercial butter used in preparation.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input, corrected erroneous cultural metadata, added multilingual names, ontology, GI, health context, and LLM training sections.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'close' to 'closest_parent'; added -1 field documentation to notes section to address g3_numeric_hygiene failures.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes section to individually document every -1 field with specific reason and source consulted, resolving all g3_numeric_hygiene failures identified in validator feedback. Each -1 field now has an explicit statement that the data is not reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy waffle entries or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024.
